segments.text: After Ahab died, Moab rebelled against Israel.
Ahaziah had fallen through the lattice-work of his upper room in Samaria and had hurt himself badly. So he sent out messengers, telling them:
Go and ask Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron, whether I will get better from this injury.
segments.speaker: Ahaziah

footnotes.text: + Ahab's son who had succeeded him as king of Israel.
+ Lattice-work: perhaps the shade used to cover a window.

segments.text: But the angel of the Lord told Elijah the Tishbite,
Go and meet the messengers of the king of Samaria and ask them, ‘Is it because there's no God in Israel that you're going to ask advice from Baal-Zebub, the god of Ekron?’
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: So this is the Lord's answer: ‘You will not leave the bed on which you are lying. You're definitely going to die.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Elijah left.
The messengers returned to the king, and he asked them,
Why have you come back?
segments.speaker: Ahaziah

segments.text: A man came and met us,
segments.speaker: Messengers

segments.text: they replied.

He told us, ‘Go back to the king who sent you, and tell him, This is what the Lord says: Is it because there is no God in Israel that you are sending to ask advice from Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron? As a result you will not leave the bed on which you are lying. You're definitely going to die.’
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: What was he like, this man who met you and told you all this?
segments.speaker: Ahaziah

segments.text: the king asked.
He was a hairy man wearing a leather belt around his waist,
segments.speaker: Messengers

segments.text: they replied.
It's Elijah the Tishbite,
segments.speaker: Ahaziah

segments.text: said the king.
Then the king sent an army captain with fifty men to Elijah. The captain went up to Elijah, who was sitting on the top of a hill, and told him,
Man of God, the king orders you, ‘Come down!’
segments.speaker: Captain of Fifty

segments.text: Elijah replied to the captain,
If I am a man of God, then let fire fall from heaven and burn up you and your fifty men.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Fire fell from heaven and burned up the captain and his men.
So the king sent another captain with his fifty men to Elijah. The captain said to Elijah,
Man of God, the king orders you, ‘Come down immediately!’
segments.speaker: Second Captain of Fifty

segments.text: Elijah replied to the captain,
If I am a man of God, then let fire fall from heaven and burn up you and your fifty men.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Fire fell from heaven and burned up the captain and his men.
So the king sent a third captain with his fifty men. The third captain went up, kneeled before Elijah, and pleaded with him,
Man of God, please value my life and the lives of these fifty men.
segments.speaker: Third Captain of Fifty

segments.text: Yes, fire has fallen from heaven and burned up the first two captains of fifty, along with all their men. But now please value my life!
segments.speaker: Third Captain of Fifty

segments.text: Then the angel of the Lord told Elijah,
Go down with him. You don't have to be afraid of him.
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: So Elijah got up and went down with him to the king.
Elijah told the king,

This is what the Lord says: ‘Is it because there's no God in Israel for you to consult that you have sent messengers to ask advice of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron? As a result you will not leave the bed on which you are lying. You're definitely going to die.’
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Ahaziah died just as the Lord had said through Elijah. Because he had no son, Joram succeeded him as king in the second year of the reign of Jehoram, son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah.

footnotes.text: + 'Joram, spelled here Jehoram. For purposes of clarity the king of Israel is called Joram in this translation, while the king of Judah is called Jehoram, though the names are basically the same and are used interchangeably in the Hebrew text.

segments.text: The rest of what happened in Ahaziah's reign and what he did are recorded in the Book of Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
